description: A builder for spawning external processes. Command lets you configure and run system commands, capture their output, and check exit status. Python equivalent: subprocess.run() / subprocess.Popen.

methods.name: new
methods.description: Creates a new Command for the given program. The program is searched in PATH if not an absolute path. Python equivalent: subprocess.Popen(['program']).
methods.example:
use std::process::Command;
let mut cmd = Command::new("ls");

methods.name: arg
methods.signature: fn arg<S: AsRef<OsStr>>(&mut self, arg: S) -> &mut Command
methods.description: Adds a single argument to the command. Arguments are passed directly (no shell expansion). Can be chained.

methods.name: args
methods.signature: fn args<I, S>(&mut self, args: I) -> &mut Command where I: IntoIterator<Item = S>, S: AsRef<OsStr>
methods.description: Adds multiple arguments at once from an iterator.

methods.name: output
methods.description: Runs the command and captures stdout, stderr, and the exit status. Waits for the process to finish. Python equivalent: subprocess.run(capture_output=True).

methods.name: spawn
methods.description: Starts the command as a child process without waiting for it to finish. Returns a Child handle for managing the process. Python equivalent: subprocess.Popen().

methods.name: status
methods.description: Runs the command and returns its exit status. Inherits stdin, stdout, and stderr from the parent process.
